title: eulophia welwitschii subsp. aurantiaca
intro: Eulophia welwitschii subsp. aurantiaca, also known as the Orange-flowered Eulophia, is a sub-species of the Eulophia welwitschii orchid, a terrestrial plant native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Eulophia welwitschii subsp. aurantiaca include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
